title: caltha palustris f. atrorubra
intro: Caltha palustris f. atrorubra, also known as Marsh Marigold or Western Buttercup, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Caltha palustris f. atrorubra can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.
